name: Vía Verde del Eresma Trail
name_original: Vía Verde Valle de Eresma — ida y vuelta desde Hontanares de Eresma
description: Hike the easy 4.8-mile (7.8 km) Vía Verde del Eresma Trail, exploring diverse landscapes and old railway history near Hontanares de Eresma.
region_intro: Hiking around Hontanares De Eresma offers routes primarily situated within the Eresma river valley. The terrain features open countryside and paths that often follow the river's course, providing views of the surrounding landscape. Elevations are generally gentle, making many trails accessible for various fitness levels. The area also incorporates historical elements, particularly near Segovia, which is often included in local hiking routes. Best hiking trails around Hontanares De Eresma The most popular hiking route is Ciudad de Segovia: the Aqueduct and the Alcázar — circular, a 2.9 miles (4.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 18 minutes to complete. This trail combines urban exploration with views of historical landmarks. Another top favourite among local hikers is Río Eresma with views of the Alcázar de Segòvia — circular, a moderate 4.9 miles (7.9 km) path. This route follows the Eresma river, offering scenic views of the Alcázar de Segovia. Local hikers also love the Vía Verde del Eresma Trail, a 4.8 miles (7.8 km) trail leading through converted railway paths, often completed in about 1 hour 58 minutes. Hiking around Hontanares De Eresma is defined by river valleys, open countryside, and historical points of interest. The network offers options for different ability levels, including easy hikes Hontanares De Eresma. The routes in Hontanares De Eresma are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 700 hikers have used komoot to explore Hontanares De Eresma's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Hontanares De Eresma?
A: There are over 45 hiking routes around Hontanares De Eresma, offering a variety of options for different skill levels and preferences. The komoot community has explored these trails over 700 times.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Hontanares De Eresma?
A: Yes, Hontanares De Eresma offers a good selection of easy hikes. With 22 routes classified as easy, beginners can enjoy gentle terrain, often following the Eresma river or exploring historical areas. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1153178169/via-verde-del-eresma-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde del Eresma Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is 7.8 km long and follows a converted railway path.

Q: Can I find family-friendly hiking routes in Hontanares De Eresma?
A: Many of the easy trails in Hontanares De Eresma are suitable for families. The gentle elevations and well-defined paths, particularly those along the Eresma river or through urban historical areas, make them enjoyable for all ages. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1153091765/ciudad-de-segovia-the-aqueduct-and-the-alcazar-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ciudad de Segovia: the Aqueduct and the Alcázar — circular&lt;/a&gt; route is a great option, combining urban exploration with historical sights over 4.6 km.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails around Hontanares De Eresma?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on most hiking trails in the open countryside around Hontanares De Eresma, provided they are kept under control. When hiking near urban areas or protected natural spaces, it's always best to check local regulations regarding leashes and waste disposal. Many routes along the Eresma river are popular with dog walkers.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the Hontanares De Eresma area?
A: Yes, several popular routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1153091765/ciudad-de-segovia-the-aqueduct-and-the-alcazar-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ciudad de Segovia: the Aqueduct and the Alcázar — circular&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1153077335/rio-eresma-with-views-of-the-alcazar-de-segovia-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Eresma with views of the Alcázar de Segòvia — circular&lt;/a&gt;, which offer varied scenery and historical views.

Q: What historical landmarks or attractions can I see while hiking near Hontanares De Eresma?
A: The region is rich in history. Many trails offer views of or pass by significant landmarks. You can see the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/778047" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aqueduct of Segovia&lt;/a&gt;, the majestic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5200969" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Segovia Cathedral&lt;/a&gt;, and the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1814018" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcázar of Segovia&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3532895" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Hontanares de Eresma Station&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6315593" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Lázaro Bridge&lt;/a&gt; are also notable points of interest.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Hontanares De Eresma?
A: The trails in Hontanares De Eresma are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 60 reviews. Hikers often praise the scenic river views, the historical points of interest, and the accessibility of many routes for various fitness levels.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Hontanares De Eresma?
A: The best time to hike in Hontanares De Eresma is typically during spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) when the weather is mild and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summers can be hot, especially in the open countryside, while winters are generally mild but can be chilly, with occasional frost.

Q: Are there options for longer, more challenging hikes in the region?
A: While many trails are easy to moderate, there are options for longer hikes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10416054" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Segovia Cathedral – Aqueduct of Segovia loop from Segovia&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 7.7 km route with some elevation gain. The region offers 23 moderate trails, providing more extended walks through the river valleys and countryside.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Hontanares De Eresma?
A: The terrain around Hontanares De Eresma primarily features open countryside and paths that often follow the Eresma river's course. You can expect gentle elevations, making most trails accessible. Some routes incorporate urban sections, especially near Segovia, with paved paths, while others are natural dirt trails.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in Hontanares De Eresma?
A: Parking is generally available in and around the villages and towns that serve as starting points for hikes. For routes closer to Segovia, public parking lots are usually accessible. It's advisable to check specific route descriptions on komoot for detailed parking information near your chosen trailhead.

Q: Are there any trails that specifically follow the Eresma river?
A: Yes, the Eresma river is a central feature of the region's hiking landscape. Many trails closely follow its course, offering picturesque riverside views. A notable example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1153077335/rio-eresma-with-views-of-the-alcazar-de-segovia-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Eresma with views of the Alcázar de Segòvia — circular&lt;/a&gt;, which provides scenic riverside walking with historical backdrops.
tips: Originally it was called Fontanares due to the abundance of water in its surroundings, probably related to its proximity to the river Eresma from which it receives its surname. Today it is a mostly residential municipality but it retains a peasant spirit due to its location in the Segovian countryside region and its still active agricultural activity.

The Hermitage of Our Lady of Las Vegas, located in Los Huertos, Segovia, is a building with a rich history.
The hermitage can be dated to the 11th century, its plan is basilica-type, with three naves divided by pillars and attached columns.
The hermitage is located in a picturesque environment, near the banks of the Eresma River and next to the Camino de Santiago.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Vía Verde del Eresma Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail primarily features a compacted earth surface, making for a smooth and gentle walk. You'll also encounter some asphalted sections and occasional rocky parts, but overall, the gradient is very gentle as it follows an old railway line.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect to see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll experience diverse and picturesque landscapes. These include riverine forests, poplars, holm and Pyrenean oak forests, and pine forests. The trail also passes through vast cereal fields, offering views reminiscent of the 'Campos de Castilla', and from certain points, you can even see the Sierra de Guadarrama. Keep an eye out for historical elements like old railway stations and bridges.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or viewpoints along the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route offers several points of interest. You can see the Old Hontanares de Eresma Station and the Ermita de Las Vegas. Other historical remnants include old railway bridges, a 176-meter-long tunnel (Perogordo tunnel) near Segovia, and various miradores (viewpoints) that provide unique vistas of the Segovian and Valladolid pine lands.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for hiking. However, it's always advisable to check for current conditions before planning your trip, as sections have occasionally faced temporary closures due to vegetation or maintenance issues.

faqs.question: Is the Vía Verde del Eresma Trail suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, it is generally considered easy to moderate, with many sections explicitly described as having 'scarce difficulty'. Its gentle gradients, a characteristic of former railway lines, make it ideal for families and beginners looking for an accessible outdoor experience.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is part of the official Vía Verde del Eresma and Vía Verde del Valle del Eresma networks. It also intersects with sections of the Camino Natural del Eresma and the Madrid Way of Saint James.

faqs.question: Is the trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While not explicitly stated in the provided information, Vías Verdes (greenways) in Spain are generally dog-friendly. It's usually expected that dogs are kept on a leash and that owners clean up after them.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to use the trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to access the Vía Verde del Eresma Trail. It is a public greenway open for recreational use.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The trail passes through various towns and former railway stations, many of which offer parking options. For example, starting from Hontanares de Eresma, you can typically find parking near the old station areas or within the town. Look for designated parking spots in towns along the route.

faqs.question: Are there any rest areas or facilities along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail includes various rest areas, some conveniently located near former railway stations. These spots provide opportunities to pause, relax, and enjoy the surroundings.

faqs.question: What should I be aware of regarding current trail conditions?
faqs.answer: It's important to check for current conditions before your visit. Historically, sections of the greenway, such as between Olmedo and Coca, and between Armuña and Olmedo, have experienced temporary transit problems due to vegetation or maintenance. Always look for recent updates from local authorities or trail websites.
